# SINBI MUAY THAI, PHUKET, THAILAND

Sinbi Muay Thai Camp in Phuket Thailand is a traditional gym that focuses on providing excellence in Muay Thai training. Its location is near the most beautiful beach in Phuket. Nai Harn Beach makes it one of the most desirable gyms on the island to visit and train at.

Sinbi Muay Thai gym exists to support and train our young Thai fighters as well as to provide real Muay Thai training to students from other countries who wish to improve their fitness/technique. The exceptional quality of training at the gym is exemplified by the fact we regularly attract the best fighters in the sport who choose Sinbi when they have a fight South of Bangkok.

[Checkout our Promo Video](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/)

## Welcome – we are open all year round!

## About Sinbi Muay Thai

If you want **serious training**, while enjoying a **tropical holiday**, look no further than Sinbi Muay Thai. Our trainers were trained by champions, they became champions and now they’ve trained and are training numerous champions currently!

Sinbi Muay Thai Camp is a massive, open-air gym with 3 full-size rings, 30 bags including 2 wall bags, a stretching/shadowboxing, and warmup area, a fully kitted weights room which has a selection of free weights, kettlebells, benches, isolation machines, and spinning bikes, rowers and a high rise roof which allows a breeze to flow through the gym to help cool you down.

We also have the largest Muay Thai shop in Phuket stocking absolutely the best in Muay Thai equipment at the best prices.

The Sinbi Apartments is a 21 room facility located just a 300 meter walk from the camp. We offer a mix of private rooms, shared rooms as well as 2 VIP rooms and Penthouse rooms. Featuring amazing views from the rooftop terrace and a tranquil swimming pool to relax in after training, the Sinbi Apartments guarantee you have a more than comfortable stay during your time training with us.

## Facilities

- Accommodation 21 Rooms
- 3 full size, 7 m2, competition-size rings
- 30 bags
- Huge, open plan gym that uses the sea breeze to keep us cool.
- Kettlebells
- Medicine balls
- Weights room with multiple strength and conditioning machines including Smith, free weights, benches, rowing machines, spinning bikes, and more.

## Why Choose Us?

Sinbi Muay Thai camp offers authentic Muay Thai training to all levels in a friendly environment under the instruction of experienced trainers with the bonus of beautiful sea views and a cooling breeze. Sinbi is located on Phuket, Thailand’s largest island, renowned for its blue waters, sandy beaches, spicy Thai cuisine and excellent snorkelling and diving. In recent years Phuket has become a popular destination for those wanting to train MuayThai, and as a result, there is an ever-growing number of MuayThai Camps. So, why choose Sinbi?

##### LOCATION

Sinbi is on the southernmost tip of the island in the area of Rawai close to beautiful Naiharn beach. The area has grown in popularity in recent years with more cafes, places to eat and hang out popping up but still retains its village vibe. Sinbi is located in a quiet street, in an elevated position, so you train with sea and jungle views and a welcome sea breeze.

##### ALL LEVELS ARE WELCOME

Although Sinbi Muay Thai camp is well known for training elite Thai and international fighters. Sinbi welcomes anyone who would like to train Muay Thai, no matter what age or level. Whether a complete beginner or someone preparing for a fight. Both group training and private training are available.

##### AUTHENTIC MUAY THAI

If you want to learn muay Thai as a martial art in its true form then it’s important that the Muay Thai gym you select has authentic training. Sinbi offers just this – authentic training focusing on techniques, endurance, and various aspects of the sport whether you want to fight or are training for fitness and fun.

##### WE HAVE EXCEPTIONAL TRAINERS

We understand good trainers are what truly makes a good gym so Sinbi picks its trainers with care and as a result, the majority of our trainers have been at Sinbi for a long time. All of our trainers have fighting experience and most have championship belts to their name but more importantly, they are dedicated, passionate and instructive trainers who are experienced in teaching Muay Thai not only to fighters but to our international and Thai students.

##### FRIENDLY ENVIRONMENT

Sinbi owed and run by Sing, aka, Sinbi and his sister Gob manages the gym shop/office and Sinbi accommodation… BaoV Sing’s older brother is one of our veteran and well-loved trainers here. So we are always happy when our students refer to us as their “Sinbi family.”

Sinbi strives to cultivate a welcoming, healthy training environment and culture. We want our students to enjoy the experience of learning and training. Therefore, we try to encourage and challenge our students and pace the training to suit an individual’s ability and make them feel comfortable and not intimated by the training.

##### CLEANLINESS AND HYGIENE

Sinbi places a high level of importance on gym hygiene and cleanliness. Our mats are mopped, and bags and equipment and cleaned between every training session. We also have modern toilet and shower facilities on site.

+66 (0) 895 464 426 [Camp@SinbiMuayThai.com](mailto:Camp@SinbiMuayThai.com) - [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/Sinbimuaythai/) - [X](https://www.twitter.com/Sinbimuaythai/) - [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/Sinbimuaythai/) - [RSS](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/feed/) - [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/Sinbimuaythai/) - [X](https://www.twitter.com/Sinbimuaythai/) - [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/Sinbimuaythai/) - [RSS](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/feed/) # Meet The Team ## Sing Owner/Founder of Sinbi Muay Thai ![](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Sinbi-boss.jpeg) #### **Titles:** South Thailand Champion at 70lb #1 Ranked fighter at Lumpinee #1 Ranked fighter at Ratchadamnoen 2005 KOMA Champion, Korea Watch Sing’s highlight reels from back when he fought K1 in Korea: [Sinbi Taewoong HL](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lAh7JpSvaYI) [Sinbi Taewoong HL](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pi8m-PjysJE) Thopadak Wanchalerm is Sing’s Thai fighting name, which he used for many years in Bangkok. Sinbi Taewoong is Sing’s Korean fighting name, which he used when fighting K-1. Sinbi means ‘mysterious’ in Korean, and Taewoong is the name of the gym where he trained. The owner of this gym brought him to Korea, where he dominated the K1 Circuit. Born in the Songkhla Province in southern Thailand, Sing grew up with two brothers and one sister. He started training Muay Thai at the age of 9. His father taught him and his brothers at home, who had been a fighter himself and subsequently became a rice grower. His sister (who now works at Sinbi Muay Thai) often helped by holding the pads for her brothers when they were young. His mother worked as a fruit tree farmer. Sing had his first fight at the age of 9. He was South Thailand Champion at 70lbs. At 13, Sing left his home and moved to Bangkok with a friend to live and train there (Loog-tap-agat gym). His manager there was Wanpaded, a Racthadermnoen Champion. At the same time as training, he went to school and finished high school at 19. He went to University afterwards to study Sports Science and graduated at 24. Sing trained at the same gym and fought over 100 fights in Bangkok during this time. Sing fought in Bangkok’s famous and prestigious Lumpinee and Ratchadamnoen stadiums, where only the best fighters compete. He was ranked number 1 at both these stadiums. At the age of 25, Sing went to live and work in Korea for 6 months as a Muay Thai trainer, and he fought for Taewoong Muay Thai gym in Seoul. The following year Sing moved to Phuket to work as a trainer at the gym of a friend he went to school with. While training many foreign Muay Thai students and tourists, Sing started learning English. In 2005 Sing went to England to fight and teach Muay Thai in Birmingham at Trojan Gym. In 2006 Sing had a fight in Sydney, Australia, and fighting in a KOMA event in Korea where he had three fights in one day in Seoul. He had fought the KOMA event previously in 2005 and won the event. The first time Sing was fighting in a K-1 MAX event in Busan, Korea, in February 2006. His second K-1 fight was in the K-1 KHAN Fighting Network in Seoul, Korea, in September 2006, where he won against the Mongolian fighter ByamBa by KO in the 2nd round. In total, Sing has ten Championship belts to his name. He opened Sinbi Muay Thai Camp in January 2007. ## OUR TRAINERS The Muay Thai training offered at Sinbi is second to none in Phuket. Most of our trainers have fought at a very high level at Lumpinee or Rajadamnern stadiums in Bangkok and internationally. However, what sets our trainers apart is that they all have experience training our international and Thai students at different levels and abilities. In addition, all of our trainers can communicate in good enough English to teach and instruct our students. Sinbi handpicks its trainers to make sure they are a good fit for the team and are happy in their job. Our trainers know how to challenge students and adapt the pace of training to suit different abilities and fitness levels. Sinbi places a high emphasis on teaching the basics – stance, movement, balance, and correct technique. This will make strikes easier to perform effectively. Muay Thai should look beautiful too. You may hear your trainer saying “mai suay” – not beautiful and if you are lucky “suay” – beautiful during training. ### PRIVATE SESSIONS If you’re currently training at Sinbi, feel free to schedule private sessions directly with any trainer—just confirm your booking and make payment at the gym shop. You can also book in advance via email, WhatsApp, Instagram, or Facebook DMs. Please note: _We are closed on Sundays and do not offer training that day._ Different trainers have different fighting styles, which is reflected in how and what they teach. We encourage you to work with a variety of trainers to experience their unique techniques and tricks. Private sessions are highly recommended while you’re here, as they offer a more personalized experience and allow you to focus on areas you want to improve or practice. ![](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Sinbi-trainer-Fa.jpg) ## **FA** Head Trainer \| Fight Name: Yod Khompatak \| Style: Muay Femur Fa is our calm, technical southpaw with over 160 fights since age 10. His smooth padwork sharpens timing, balance, and technique. Patient and clear, he adapts lessons to beginners and pros alike. Fa’s teaching style is precise but friendly—he breaks down complex moves so you don’t feel lost in the muay thai jungle. Still actively fighting, he leads with quiet confidence. Bonus: watch him take on the legendary Pornsane Sitmonchai to see his skills in action! ![](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Saenkaeng-1.jpg) ## **SAENG KAENG** Trainer \| Fight Name: Saenkeang Kaitjarunchai \| Style: Muay Khao Keang, from Songkhla, started fighting at 5 and has 225+ fights. A clinch master, he’s great at teaching knees, elbows, and sweeps with playful energy. His hands-on, clear teaching style makes tough clinch moves easier to learn. Expect a motivating, fun session that pushes your limits but always keeps a smile on your face. Serious fighters and newbies love his mix of challenge and cheer. ![](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Met-1.jpg) ## **MET** Trainer \| Fight Name: Thongdeenoi Sit Krumai \| Style: Muay Femur Met is an old-school, no-nonsense trainer who pushes you hard in training but smiles when you least expect it. He’s coached elite fighters (including gym owner Sinbi Taewoong) and excels in technical pad work. Though recovering from a stroke and no longer holding pads, he’s back at Sinbi cheering everyone on and quietly guiding fighters with his sharp eye and calm presence. ![](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-BaoV-2.jpg) ## **BAO V** Trainer \| Fight Name: Yodlam Singsonchai \| Style: Muay Femur BaoV, Sinbi Taewwong’s older brother and “Mr. Fixer” of the gym, is calm and methodical. Perfect for beginners and advanced students, he focuses on stance, rhythm, and timing—encouraging relaxed, efficient movement. BaoV’s patient approach helps reduce wasted effort and injury risk. Known for spotting details others miss, he’s great for anyone returning from injury or wanting to refine their technique with a steady guide. ![](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Boa.jpg) ### Bowie Trainer \| Fight Name: Bovy Chor Walachai \| Style: Muay Khao Bovy has years of experience training in popular Thai islands and speaks excellent English. He’s approachable, always smiling, and great for fighters who want clear, no-nonsense instruction. If you like fun sessions but don’t want sloppy technique to sneak in, Bovy’s your guy. Plus, he runs a nearby restaurant with tasty food — training and a good meal? Win-win. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/Fathis.png) ### Fatis Trainer \| Fight Name: Pethuasai \| Style: Muay Khao/Femur Fatis, Fa’s younger brother, fights with technical skill and high energy. His fast padwork keeps you moving and motivated. Friendly with a great sense of humor, he’s always watching to give sharp, useful feedback. His English has improved a lot since joining Sinbi — so expect clear, fun, and fast-paced sessions. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Bao.png) ### Bao Trainer \| Fight Name: Saoeak M.U.Den \| Style: Muay Khao Bao is the “gentle giant” from Surat Thani, with 250 fights and lots of patience. He adjusts training to your fitness and skill, building strengths while tackling weaknesses. His calm, easy-going style makes even tough workouts manageable. As the tallest trainer, he’s ideal for bigger students looking for solid pad work and personal attention. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/Trainer_Dee.png) ### Dee Trainer \| Fight Name: Kimchi \| Style: Muay Femur Dee, from Songkla, is a technical pro with a sharp eye for detail. Fluent in English, he has coached internationally and brings a relaxed yet focused vibe to training. Dee makes learning enjoyable and effective, whether you’re just starting or mastering advanced skills. His friendly humor keeps sessions fun while ensuring you improve every round. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-YaYa.jpg) ### YaYa Trainer \| Fight Name: Tewaraj \| Style: Muay Femur Yaya has 200+ fights and a cheerful, easy-going coaching style. He enjoys challenging students by throwing fast strikes during pads to test reflexes and guard. Loved by beginners and veterans alike, Yaya helps you sharpen defense and stay alert, all while keeping training lively and upbeat. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Soleh.jpg) ### Soleh Trainer \| Fight Name: Plaimin Lukbaanyai \| Style: Muay Femur Soleh from Songkla is a master of clear, easy-to-follow teaching. With years of experience in Thailand and Southeast Asia, he breaks down techniques simply and controls pad pace to develop speed, power, and timing. Also a Muay Boran expert, Soleh adds traditional flair to his modern coaching. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fon-3.png) ### Fon Trainer \| Fight Name: Fon Luang \| Style: Muay  Femur Fon, from Yala, learned Muay Thai from his father and fights with calm precision. He’s especially great with kids and beginners, focusing on confidence, timing, and rhythm. His clear, patient coaching helps students develop fluid, controlled technique in a supportive environment. With fight experience in Thailand and Malaysia, Fon’s steady guidance makes training both fun and productive. ![](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/sinbi_trainer_leo.png) ### Leo Trainer \| Fight Name:  PatakSeua  \| Style: Muay Femur Leo, Sing/Sinbi’s nephew, grew up in the gym, literally learning Muay Thai from his nappies. Now a confident, precise pad holder, he coaches all levels with calm maturity beyond his years. Leo is a rising star trainer, balancing energy and focus perfectly. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Kru_Beer_Sinbi_Trainer.png) ### Beer Trainer \| Fight Name: Petchnamchai \| Style: Muay Femur Beer fought fast and trains fast. His quick, tricky padwork tests your cardio and defense with feints and tricks. Serious about fight training, he’s tough but fair, pushing you to improve technique and reaction. He’s fought and coached internationally in Australia and China, bringing a professional edge and playful challenge to every session. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Top.jpeg) ### Top Trainer \| Fight Name: Ninmongkon \| Style: Muay Femur Top, from Satun, is a patient, adaptable trainer with a smooth pad flow. Fighting in Thailand, Malaysia, and Japan gave him a broad perspective, which he uses to teach sweeps and correct mistakes gently but firmly. Beginners and advanced students appreciate his comfortable, judgment-free coaching.

![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Pick.jpeg) ### Pick Trainer \| Fight Name: Petchdam Kiatkorwit \| Style: Muay Femur Pick is calm and approachable, matching pad pace to your level. With years fighting and training experience in Surat Thani and Koh Samui, he’s great at patiently breaking down moves and adapting sessions. He’s your reliable, steady trainer who never rushes and always makes sure you get it right. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Sinbi_trainer_Ja.png) ### Ja Trainer \| Fight Name: Ja Sinbimuaythai \| Style: Muay Femur (aggressive) Ja is a world champ with 155 fights and a big personality. Friendly and chatty, he’s got a great sense of humor and excellent English. His aggressive style favors knees, and he’s brilliant at fast combos. Warning: if there’s a camera nearby, Ja cranks up the intensity—no slow days with this guy!

![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Speed.png) ### Speed Trainer \| Fight Name: Yodspeed Torsanchai \| Style: Muay Femur Speed, from Phatthalung, is all about head movement, footwork, and defense. A gold medalist and national champ, he builds padwork from simple to lightning-fast combos focusing on speed, accuracy, and power. Patient but firm, Speed pushes you hard, making him a favorite for serious fight training with a creative twist. . ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/26.png) ### Gun Trainer \| Fight Name: Ekkarat Wehadgym \| Style: Muay Khao Gun loves to challenge your cardio with fast-paced pad rounds. Known for making you double your kick counts, he keeps sessions lively and focused. His playful, chatty style balances hard work with fun, helping you refine technique without burning out. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/Trainer_Dew.png) ### Dew Trainer \| Fight Name: Andrew P. Borirak \| Style: Muay Femur Dew began fighting at 4 and has about 200 bouts. A fast pad worker with a positive attitude, he motivates all levels with his cheerful style. Experienced in Malaysia and Thailand, he enjoys sharing his technical skills and keeping training upbeat and productive. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Eyat-1.png) ### Chocolate (Eyat) Trainer \| Style: Muay Femur Chocolate is an “old school” coach from Krabi who focuses on footwork and rhythm to set up strikes and defense. His fun, high-energy style makes training enjoyable and approachable for all levels. He’s known for making even complex techniques feel relaxed and achievable. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Korat.png) ### Korat Trainer \| Fight Name: Korat Noi (Robocop) \| Style: Muay Mat (punches) Korat is a calm, positive coach from Isaan with a knack for adapting training to all levels. His creative pad work balances intensity and ease, with subtle corrections that build confidence. Known for his friendly smile, Korat ensures every session is enjoyable and stress-free. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Vee.png) ### Vee Trainer \| Style: Muay Femur Vee from Suratthani brings technical grace and experience. Friendly and approachable, he creates a supportive space for all levels. His thoughtful coaching helps you develop skillful, precise technique, and his international fight experience adds depth to his lessons. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/sinbi_trainer-nai.png) ### Nai Trainer \| Fight Name: Patakin Sinbi Muay Thai \| Style: Muay Femur Nai, from Nakhon Si Thammarat, is quick and deceptively strong. Former elite fighter and now a growing trainer, he’s friendly, focused, and adapts well to all levels. He understands exactly what fighters need to succeed and shares that with care and precision.

![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/32.png) ### WILLIAM Trainer \| Fight Name: Liamphet Wang Muen 5 Stars William has 300 fights under his belt and a lively, energetic teaching style. His positive attitude and patience make training fun and effective for all levels. Competent in English and always eager to learn, he balances slowing down for beginners with pushing advanced students hard. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/sinbi_trainer_m.png) ### M Trainer \| Fight Name: M is a calm, reliable trainer focused on angles, timing, and distance. Friendly but serious, he creates a supportive atmosphere that suits all levels and ages. Whether building fundamentals or fight IQ, training with M sharpens your movement and pad skills steadily. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/sinbi_trainer_petch.png) ### Petch Trainer \| Fight Name: Patalek Sinbi Muay Thai Petch is a patient, steady trainer ideal for bigger students and beginners alike. Known for solid technique and quiet confidence, he provides clear corrections and reliable coaching that helps everyone progress at their own pace. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/sinbi_trainer_bas.png) ### Bas Trainer \| Fight Name: “Crazy Bas” from Chumphon matches his big personality with high-energy, playful pad sessions. He focuses on defense and keeps you on your toes by throwing unexpected kicks and punches. Training with Bas is challenging, fun, and full of laughs. ![Sinbi-Muay-Thai-Trainer-Fathis](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/sinbi_trainer_la.png) ### La /SEEJA Trainer \| Style: Muay Femur Trainer La from Songkla teaches fast, precise strikes and evasive movement with calm patience. He breaks down complex techniques simply and supports steady progress from beginner to advanced. His passion and supportive attitude help students build confidence and skill.

## OUR FIGHTERS Sinbi Muay Thai prides itself on supporting and developing young Thai fighters. Our aim is to give them all the necessary skills and guidance they need to become future champions. With our Nak Muay’s fighting regularly at stadiums all over Thailand, as well as at the prestigious Rajadamnern and Lumpinee stadiums, we have developed a strong reputation for producing top level fighters. ![SAM A](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_fighter_SamA-scaled.jpeg) #### SAM A Fighting name: SAM A ![JENNARong](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/1.jpg) #### JENNARong Fighting name: PATAKTHEP ![JUDY Humber](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/3.jpg) #### JUDY Humber Fighting name: Patakpalin ![NINE](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_fighter_Nine.jpeg) #### NINE Fighting name: PATAKNIN ![TONG](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_figher_Tong.jpeg) #### TONG Fighting name: PatakEK ![DEW](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_fighter_Dew-scaled.jpeg) #### DEW Fighting name: PATAKCAM ![AFRAN](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_fighter_Afran-scaled.jpeg) #### AFRAN Fighting name: PatakLEELA ![DAWUD](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_fighter_Dawud-scaled.jpeg) #### DAWUD Fighting name: DAWUD ![PATAKMUSLIM](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_fighter_PatakMusoim-scaled.jpeg) #### PATAKMUSLIM Fighting name: paTAKMUSLIM ![BUSTER](https://www.sinbimuaythai.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Sinbi_fighter_Buster-scaled.jpeg) #### BUSTER Fighting name: PATAKNOI

# SINBI MUAY THAI, PHUKET, THAILAND

There are 2 group training sessions per day, from Mondays to Saturdays. Morning training is from 7:30 am to 9:30 am and afternoon training is from 4:00 pm to 6:00 pm. Sundays are rest days, so the gym is closed, there is no group or private training, or use of the fitness room or gym floor.

There is no need to book group training.  You can show up on the day. Our group training is open to all ages and levels, from complete beginners to fighters and is adapted to suit different levels and abilities. Beginners will work with one or more trainers before proceeding to pad work and bag work. They will learn the basics first and then work on what they have learned during pad work, one-on-one, with their trainer.

Children are welcome to join our group classes, but please note that we do not offer classes specifically for children and require guardians to be in attendance.

Private training is available between group session times from 9.30 am to 3 pm and after 6 pm, except on Sundays (rest day). Private training needs to be booked in advance. Private sessions are a great way to speed your overall learning curve and work on any areas you feel you would like help with, from technique to sparring.

## Training Schedule

Whether you are a fighter, a trainer, a fitness enthusiast or just someone who wants to get in shape or lose a few pounds, Sinbi Muay Thai will give you the results you want, and an experience that you will never forget.

Every student gets 5 x 3 rounds of pad work time with one of the trainers during the group class.

\\* Muay Thai sparring or boxing sparring and clinching takes place most days- more often than not in the afternoon session.

\\* Every Saturday morning students are taught the Wai Kru by the trainers.

Training Days

Mon – Sat

Afternoon Training

Sundays Closed – No Group or Private training. Stricly no use of fitness room or gym floor

#### WHAT DOES A TYPICAL MUAY THAI CLASS LOOK LIKE?

**Running:** Running is not mandatory (unless you are preparing to fight) but strongly suggested.

**Warm-Up:** Running on mats, skipping, followed by stretching.

**Shadow Boxing:** whilst shadow boxing, the trainers will observe and start correcting your technique and gauge what sort of level you are. Shadowboxing improves footwork, coordination, technique visualisation, cardio and conditioning.

**Pad work:** Here at Sinbi, you always get 5 rounds lasting 3 minutes each of hitting pads with the trainers, developing correct technique, speed, power and stamina.

**Technique:** Instruction and correction on various styles and combinations by the trainers.

**Bag work:** At least 5 rounds of working techniques on a variety of different bags.

**Clinching**

**Muay Thai/boxing sparring.**

**Conditioning:** Drills on the bag, push-ups, sit-ups etc

Cooldown stretching

## **Pad Holding Certification Course**

The course consists of **10 hours of instruction**, scheduled flexibly between group training sessions during your stay.

The trainer will go through the **fundamentals of pad holding**, including:

- Correct stance and positioning
- Proper pad alignment for kicks, punches, knees & elbows
- Building combinations safely & effectively
- Managing distance, balance & movement
- Timing, rhythm & communication with students/ fighters
- Energy conservation & safety for both pad holder and fighter

In addition, the trainer will be happy to cover any specific areas you feel you need help with. Completion of the 10 hours is required to receive the **Sinbi Muay Thai Pad Holding Certificate**.

# SINBI MUAY THAI Accommodation

Our accommodation, Sinbi Apartments, is located a hop, skip, and jump away ( 300 metres about a 3-minute walk ) from the gym, in a quiet residential street that runs parallel to the gym. You can roll out of bed and be at the gym and ready for training in a matter of minutes. It is conveniently close to local amenities such as convenience stores, restaurants, cafes, and massage studios.

The Sinbi Apartments is a 21 room facility with a mix of private rooms and 2 VIP rooms, and a Penthouse room. Featuring amazing views from the shared rooftop terrace and a tranquil swimming pool to relax in after training, the Sinbi Apartments guarantee you have a more than comfortable stay during your time training with us. We have four types of rooms to suit different budgets. All our rooms are outfacing, spacious bright, with modern furnishing and ensuite bathrooms.

# SINBI MUAY THAI, FAQ

These are some of the most commonly asked questions we get asked. If we can assist you in any way, please let us know. If you have a question that’s not on here, please feel free to contact us or jump on our Discord and ask us there.

[E-Mail Us](mailto:%20Camp@sinbi-muaythai.com)

[Discord](https://discord.gg/n77fbUEtJZ)

##### What skills do I need before training at Sinbi?

### 🥊 Do I need to be fit or have experience to train at Sinbi?

**Not at all!** You don’t need any previous experience or special skills to train with us. At Sinbi, we welcome **everyone — from complete beginners to professional fighters**. Our trainers are experienced in working with all levels and will make sure you learn the basics before progressing to more advanced techniques.

* * *

### 💡 New to training or just getting back into shape?

If you’re not currently very active or are just starting out, we **recommend beginning with one session per day**. This will help you **acclimatize to the heat, intensity, and new movements** without overwhelming your body.

If you feel tired during a session — **no problem at all**. Just take a break, sip some water, and rest as needed. No one will mind. Listening to your body is important, especially when you’re adjusting to a new training routine or environment.

##### What are the training prices?

There are many different training options and packages to choose from. [Please check out our Pricing section.](http://www.sinbi-muaythai.com/prices.html)

##### Where can I stay while training at Sinbi?

Options. [See our Accommodation Page for details.](http://www.sinbi-muaythai.com/accommodation.php)

##### What should I bring with me?

### What if I don’t have my own gear?

If you don’t have gloves or shin guards, don’t worry — you can rent them at our gym shop:

- **Glove rental**: 50 THB per session

- **Shin guard rental**: 100 THB per session


However, we strongly **encourage you to bring your own equipment** to minimize the risk of bacterial infections such as staph.

For those **not sparring**, the **essential gear** is:

- **Boxing gloves**

- **Hand wraps** (not available for rent)


Hand wraps are **available for purchase** at our shop for **450 THB**. If you’re only doing one session, you may train without wraps, though we still recommend using them for wrist support and hygiene.

For those who are **sparring**, you will need:

- Your own **gloves**, **shin guards**, **hand wraps**, **groin guard**, and **mouthguard**

- We provide **16oz gloves** for sparring purposes only


All necessary gear is available for sale in our shop.

**Additional recommendations**:

- Wear **loose, comfortable clothing** suitable for movement

- Bring **running shoes** if you’d like to join our pre-training runs


##### Do you have a gear shop? Prices? What’s available?

Yes, we do! Our **on-site gear shop** has everything you need for training — whether you’re just getting started or looking to upgrade your kit.

We stock a variety of high-quality Muay Thai gear from **Sinbi**, **Twins**, **Top King**, and **IFS**. Stock and availability may vary from week to week, and prices can differ slightly depending on brand, style, and glove weight.

#### 🔹 **Gear & Apparel Price Guide:**

**Gloves**

- Prices start at **2,300 THB**


**Shin Guards**

- Prices start at **2,700 THB**


**Muay Thai Shorts**

- **Twins & IFS**: 1,800 THB


**T-Shirts & Singlets**

- **Sinbi T-shirts & Singlets**: 500 THB

- **Sinbi Boxing Stadium T-shirt**: 800 THB

- **Sinbi Sleeveless Hoodies**: 800 THB

- **IFS Singlets**: 1,000 THB


We also carry other essentials like **hand wraps, mouthguards, groin protectors**, and more — everything you need to train safely and comfortably.

If you’re unsure what to buy, our trainers or staff will be happy to help you choose the right gear for your needs!

##### Do you rent scooters? How much do you charge?

### 🛵 Scooter Rentals & Safety Guidelines

#### **Rental Rates & Deposit:**

We offer convenient scooter rentals with a required **2,000 THB security deposit**.

**125cc Scooters:**

- 250 THB per day

- 1,500 THB per week

- 4,000 THB per month


**150cc Scooters:**

- 350 THB per day

- 2,150 THB per week

- 5,500 THB per month


* * *

#### **Important Safety Guidelines:**

- **International Driving Permit (IDP):**

We highly recommend obtaining an IDP before arriving in Thailand. It is legally required to drive and helps avoid fines.

➤ [Department of Land Transport (Thailand)](https://www.dlt.go.th/en/international-driving-licence)

- **Helmet Use:**

Wearing a helmet is mandatory by law. No exceptions.

- **Appropriate Attire:**

Do not ride shirtless. Fines for riding without a shirt or without a valid license **start at 1,000 THB**.

- **Footwear:**

Always wear proper footwear — avoid flip-flops or sandals when riding.


* * *

#### **Insurance Information:**

While basic accident coverage is typically included in scooter rentals, it often does **not cover third-party damage or injuries** to the rider. For extra protection, consider these recommended providers:

- ➤ [Roojai Motorbike Insurance](https://www.roojai.com/en/motorbike-insurance/)

- ➤ [AA Insurance Brokers Thailand](https://aainsure.net/bike-insurance.html)

- ➤ [CheckDi Motorcycle Insurance](https://checkdi.com/th/motorbike/main?lg=en)


We strongly recommend checking whether your **travel insurance** covers scooter use, or purchasing a **dedicated motorbike insurance policy**.

* * *

#### **Safety Note:**

> ⚠️ The **majority of accidents in Phuket are scooter-related**, not training-related.
>
> Ride carefully and responsibly at all times.

* * *

### 🛂 International Driving Permit (IDP) Information

An **IDP is essential** for legally driving in Thailand. You must carry it **along with your national driver’s license and passport** while operating a vehicle.

#### **Where to Get an IDP:**

**🇹🇭 Thailand**

- ➤ [Department of Land Transport](https://www.dlt.go.th/en/international-driving-licence)


**🇺🇸 USA**

- ➤ [AAA – Apply for an IDP](https://www.aaa.com/vacation/idpf.html)

- ➤ [AATA – IDP Application](https://www.aataidp.com/)


**🇬🇧 United Kingdom**

- ➤ GOV.UK – IDP Information & Application

Apply at your local Post Office. Cost is approx. £5.50.


**🇫🇷 France**

- ➤ ANTS – Apply Online (in French)


**🇩🇪 Germany**

- Apply at your local Fahrerlaubnisbehörde (Driver’s License Office)


**🇮🇹 Italy**

- Apply through your local Motorizzazione Civile


**🇪🇸 Spain**

- Apply at the [Dirección General de Tráfico (DGT)](https://www.dgt.es/)


**🌐 Online Option**

- ➤ [E-ITA – International Driver’s License Online](https://e-ita.org/thailand/)


##### Where can I eat?

There are plenty of places to eat within a 3km radius from the gym and from the Sinbi Apartments, catering for all tastes and budgets. Healthy western food is readily available as is local Thai food.

##### Where are you located?

Located in the Rawai  area,  Sinbi is one of the best locations of any gym in Thailand. We are 3km from one of the most scenic beaches (Nai Harn) on the Island and just a 10/15 minute scooter ride from Kata beach/ town.

##### How can I fight for Sinbi?

If you are going to represent and fight for Sinbi, we need to see you train hard and give us everything you have on a daily basis. The trainers will monitor your training and will then decide if you are able to fight or not. Typically fights are confirmed a minimum of 2 weeks in advance so unfortunately if you are training with us for a duration shorter than that you will not be able to fight.

##### Can I train just 1 session and how much does it cost?

Sure you can! You are welcome to come along to any of the sessions. The cost is 600 thb per class.

##### What does a typical Muay Thai class look like?

**Running:** Running is not mandatory (unless you are preparing to fight) but strongly suggested.

**Warm-Up:** Running on mats, skipping, followed by stretching.

**Shadow Boxing:** whilst shadow boxing the trainers will observe and start correcting your technique and gauge what sort of level you are at. Shadowboxing improves footwork, coordination, technique visualization, cardio and conditioning.

**Pad work:** Here at Sinbi you always get 5 rounds lasting 3 minutes each of hitting pads with the trainers, working on developing correct technique, speed, power and stamina.

**Technique:** Instruction and correction on various techniques and combinations by the trainers.

**Bag work:** At least 5 rounds of working techniques on a variety of different bags.

**Clinching**

**Muay Thai and boxing sparring.**

**Conditioning:** Drills on the bag, push-ups, sit-ups etc

**Cooldown stretching**

##### Do I need a visa to visit Thailand

Whether you need a visa depends on your nationality and how long you plan to stay. Here’s a breakdown to help you plan your trip:

* * *

Thailand allows **visa-free stays of up to 30 days** for nationals from 93 countries. This is ideal for those looking to train Muay Thai for a few weeks or a couple of months.

✅ You’ll receive a 30 day stamp on arrival (if eligible).

🗓️ This can often be **extended for an additional 30 days** at a local immigration office, giving you a **total stay of up to 60 days**.

* * *

### 🌍 Tourist Visa – For Non-Visa-Exempt Countries

If you’re **not eligible for visa-free entry**, you’ll need to apply for a **Tourist Visa (TR)** at a Thai Embassy or Consulate before traveling.

This visa is also a good option if:

- You want to **secure your stay in advance**

- You plan to train for **30–90 days**

- You’re unsure about relying on visa-free policies


**Key details:**

- Valid for 60 days upon arrival

- Can usually be extended **once** for an additional 30 days at a Thai immigration office

- Gives you a **total of up to 90 days in Thailand**


✅ Most embassies allow applications online or in-person

✅ Can be used for training Muay Thai

💡 **Required documents typically include:**

- Passport with at least 6 months’ validity

- Visa application form

- Passport-sized photos

- Proof of accommodation

- Return or onward flight

- Proof of sufficient funds (bank statement)

- Optional: proof of Muay Thai training (we can provide a **booking confirmation**)


🔗 Apply via your local Thai Embassy/Consulate or the official Thai e-Visa Portal:

https://www.thaievisa.go.th/

* * *

### 🎓 Short Stay Study Visa (90 Days) – For Muay Thai Training

If you’re planning to train Muay Thai in Thailand for **up to 90 days** and want an alternative to visa-free or tourist visas, you can apply for a **Short Stay Study Visa** via the Thai E-Visa system.

This is suitable if:

- You’re staying a full 3-month period

- You want to avoid extensions or immigration office visits

- You need official proof of enrollment for your visa


💡 **Note from Sinbi Muay Thai:**

We can provide **visa support documentation** for the 90-day study visa, but require **full prepayment** for your training course.

✅ Example: 3 months of group training (2x daily) = **27,000 THB**

Once payment is received, we’ll issue a booking confirmation and visa support letter.

* * *

📌 **Important Note:**

The 60-day visa-free policy is **temporary** and may revert to 30 days without notice. Always check for updates before travel.

🔗 More info:

- Thai Consulate Visa Info

- [Thai Visa Policy Overview – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_Thailand)


* * *

### 🧑‍🎓 Long-Term Training (Over 90 Days)

For extended training stays, here are two visa options:

#### 1\. Non-Immigrant ED Visa (Education Visa)

- Designed for long-term study (language or Muay Thai)

- Initial 90-day stay, extendable up to 12 months

- Must be supported by a registered educational institution


⚠️ **Note from Sinbi Muay Thai:**

We cannot provide ED visa documentation. However, you may apply through a **language school in Phuket** and still train with us.

#### 2\. Destination Thailand Visa (DTV)

- Valid for 5 years, multiple entries

- 180 days per entry, extendable once

- For digital nomads, freelancers, wellness travelers, and Muay Thai trainees

- Requires 500,000 THB (~$14,000 USD) proof of funds


✅ **Sinbi Muay Thai is a licensed gym** under the Sports Authority of Thailand

✅ We can provide DTV visa documentation to **committed, long-term students only**

* * *

### 📄 How to Apply for Any Thai Visa

You’ll usually need:

- Passport with 6+ months’ validity

- Completed visa application

- Passport photos

- Proof of funds

- Proof of onward/return travel

- Travel insurance (recommended)

- Letter of enrollment (for Study, ED, or DTV visas)


Apply at your local Thai Embassy or through:

🔗 Thai e-Visa Portal

* * *

### ✅ Final Tips

- Check your eligibility for visa-free entry

- Visa policies can change — always verify before travel

- If unsure, contact your nearest Thai Embassy or Consulate

- **Sinbi Muay Thai only supports DTV and Short Stay Study Visas** for genuine, pre-paid students

## Frequently Asked Questions (F.A.Q)

##### IS THERE PARKING?

Yeah, the Sinbi Muay Thai Stadium has plenty of car and bike parking. There are ushers there to help you navigate to empty parking spaces.

##### Stadium Private Bookings?

##### IS THERE A TAXI SERVICE?

Yes, there is a Taxi service to and from the stadium. If you wish to book a Taxi to the Muay Thai fights, then please call: +66 (0) 895 464 426

##### Can I book online?

You can book online by contacting us via E-Mail: **[Camp@SinbiMuayThai.com](mailto:%20Camp@SinbiMuayThai.com)**

##### Can I bring my own Food & Drinks

You can buy snacks and beverages at the stadium or at the local food carts outside the stadium. But you are **NOT** allowed to bring your own food and drinks.

##### What about alchole?

We sell bears, spirits and mixers at the stadium. But you **CAN NOT** bring your own alcohol from the outside.

# SINBI DISCORD

One of the best things about travelling to Thailand and training Muay Thai is the friends you make and the people you meet. We try to contact each other via social media, but when months and years, that soon fades. But with the Phuket discord, we can all share our experiences, photos, and videos in one place. Plan trips with others, ask questions, make recommendations etc. The Phuket Discord is an excellent place for people new to travelling or who have never been to Phuket. You can ask questions about the area or enquire about visas or things to do when you are not training.

## What is Discord

Discord is an app similar to WhatsApp where people in a group can contact each other. But unlike WhatsApp, it’s far more interactive and organized. For example, you can not have 1,000 people in 1 WhatsApp group without utter chaos. Everyone would wake up to hundreds of messages every morning, and most would leave or permanently mute the group chat.

Initially designed for large gaming communities, Discord is a lot better. Phuket has its own Discord Server with multiple chat channels inside it, where everything can be categorized and organized. Like a channel to share your photos, another channel to share your videos, a recommendations channel etc. It’s straightforward to use with an intuitive UI. Apps like Discord are great for building communities and are slowly phasing out the usual online forums.

### DISCORD RULES

- No racist or sexist remarks.
- Try to avoid sensitive subjects.
- No self-promoting or similar services.
- No advertising other discord channels.
- No talking about Thai Monarchy or local politics.
- No nudity/vulgar/obscene pictures/memes/videos

### How To Use Discord?

When you join our Discord, you are given the Students Role. In addition, you can see on the right-hand side, or if you swipe to the right on the mobile app, there is a “Trainers” role and a “Staff” role. If you have a question and need the attention of a staff member, you can type @Staff. This will tag the member of staff, giving them a notification. In Discord, we call this “Pinging” to “Ping” a member of staff. For example, you can type _“@Staff is the office open on Sundays?“_. Please be aware though the staff can be busy, or your time zone can be in the middle of the night for them. So only Ping them if the question is somewhat urgent. Other students in the Discord can answer most questions.
